Wolf Freedom of Religion or Belief Victims List Country Profiles Defending Freedoms Project Publications " class="we-megamenu-nolink"> Annual Reports Contact Us Facebook Twitter YouTube Instagram Search Kazakhstan View the Kazakhstan chapters in our annual reports > Recent News Events Publications Advising Government Religious freedom conditions in Kazakhstan are poor for all religious communities, particularly Muslims who deviate from the state’s preferred interpretation of Islam. Authorities arbitrarily enforce vague and often onerous provisions of the 2011 religion law and the 2005 extremism law to penalize peaceful religious activities. The government heavily promotes and invests in interfaith engagement to distract from its ongoing religious freedom violations.
